multi_state_provision.provision: No waiver or disclaimer of reliance — no statement/questionnaire/acknowledgement may waive claims under state franchise law (including fraud in inducement) or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements

overrides.override_summary: Item 21 amended to include Hyatt Franchising, L.L.C.'s unaudited balance sheet
overrides.why_it_matters: California requires franchisor-level financial statement, not just parent company

overrides.override_summary: Release required at renewal/transfer does not apply to liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves franchisee's right to bring claims under MD franchise law even after signing renewal/transfer release

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon insolvency might not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Non-curable insolvency default has limited enforceability

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may bring action in Maryland for claims under MD Franchise Law, unless preempted by FAA
overrides.why_it_matters: Allows Maryland franchisee to litigate state franchise claims locally rather than in Illinois

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law applies to claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois choice of law for state franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Claims under Maryland Franchise Law must be brought within 3 years after grant of franchise
overrides.why_it_matters: Establishes specific statute of limitations for Maryland franchise claims

overrides.override_summary: 90 days' notice of termination (with 60 days to cure) and 180 days' notice for non-renewal required under Minn. Stat. §80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice/cure periods than FA provides — protects franchisee from rapid termination

overrides.override_summary: Release at renewal/transfer does not apply to extent prohibited by Minn. Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves franchisee's rights under Minnesota franchise law

overrides.override_summary: Minn. Rule 2860.4400J prohibits waiving jury trial, forum/remedies, liquidated damages, termination penalties, judgment notes
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provision may be unenforceable in Minnesota

overrides.override_summary: Minn. Stat. 80C.21 prohibits requiring litigation outside Minnesota
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois venue requirement for Minnesota franchisees

overrides.override_summary: ND Commissioner determined termination/liquidated damages unfair under Section 51-19-09 of ND Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provision explicitly challenged by state regulator; enforceability unclear

overrides.override_summary: Release does not apply to claims under North Dakota Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Preserves ND franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration at mutually agreed site (not automatically Chicago) to extent required by ND law
overrides.why_it_matters: May allow North Dakota franchisee to arbitrate closer to home

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota law applies to extent required by law
overrides.why_it_matters: Overrides Illinois choice of law

overrides.override_summary: Forum restriction to outside Rhode Island is void for claims under RI Franchise Investment Act §19-28.1-14
overrides.why_it_matters: Rhode Island franchisees can bring state franchise claims in RI courts

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law applies except as required by RI Franchise Investment Act
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law applies to state franchise claims

overrides.override_summary: Under VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564, unlawful to cancel franchise without reasonable cause. Grounds that don't constitute reasonable cause may not be enforceable.
overrides.why_it_matters: Some non-curable default grounds may be unenforceable in Virginia if not deemed 'reasonable cause'

overrides.override_summary: Cannot terminate prior to term except for good cause (with 30-day cure). Front matter provisions (pages 5-6) void certain unfair provisions.
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan law provides strong termination protections, but franchisor intends to enforce arbitration despite Michigan paragraph (f)

overrides.override_summary: Michigan law prohibits requiring arbitration/litigation outside the state (paragraph (f)). BUT franchisor explicitly states intention to enforce arbitration provision as written despite this.
overrides.why_it_matters: Direct conflict between Michigan franchise law and FA. Franchisor's stated intent to enforce despite state law creates uncertainty.

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse renewal without fair compensation if term <5 years and franchisee prohibited from continuing same business
overrides.why_it_matters: Applies only if term <5 years (not typical for 20-year JdV FA) and post-term restrictions exist (none here)
